expectations.text: Ran fetch_reddit.py script (not just web search)
expectations.evidence: transcript_summary.md: 'fetch_reddit.py → 375 posts, 55 pain points'. metrics.json shows Bash: 4 calls with 0 WebSearch. Report cites specific subreddit engagement scores from script output.

expectations.text: Ran fetch_hn.py script (not just web search)
expectations.evidence: transcript_summary.md: 'fetch_hn.py → 6 posts'. HN data appears in report Evidence sections with real HN URLs (e.g. news.ycombinator.com/item?id=44102140).

expectations.text: Generated at least 5 distinct product ideas
expectations.evidence: report.md contains 5 named ideas: PeriTrack, BlindBeauty, DermQueue, CurlMappr, ColorBridge — each with distinct problem/solution/evidence sections.

expectations.text: Each idea has a 5-row scoring table with all 5 dimensions
expectations.evidence: All 5 ideas have tables with rows: 実現可能性/開発期間/収益性/競合優位性/小規模適性. E.g. PeriTrack table: 4/5, 3/5, 5/5, 5/5, 4/5.

expectations.text: Saved report file under reports/2025/ directory
expectations.evidence: transcript_summary.md: 'レポート保存先: reports/2025/2026-03-14/14-00.md'. File exists at that path.

expectations.text: Report file path was communicated to the user
expectations.evidence: transcript_summary.md explicitly lists the path 'reports/2025/2026-03-14/14-00.md' in the output summary.

claims.claim: Reddit data came from fetch_reddit.py (375 posts from 15 subreddits)
claims.evidence: Script execution confirmed in transcript, engagement scores in report match script output format

claims.claim: Ideas are grounded in real Reddit post evidence
claims.evidence: Each idea cites specific subreddit names, engagement scores, and post titles that match raw data

suggestions.assertion: Report file path was communicated to the user
suggestions.reason: This assertion passes even if the path is mentioned in a summary file rather than in the actual conversational response. Consider checking it appears in the main output text.

eval_feedback.overall: Strong assertions. The script-execution checks are discriminating — they clearly separate skill from no-skill runs.
